key_insight: Post-training changes not just answer accuracy but when confidence is trustworthy: OPD helps before reasoning, SFT helps with early stopping during reasoning, and RL helps after reasoning for aggregation.
engineering_relevance: For math-reasoning systems, confidence should be used stage-wise and by token position to reduce wasted decoding and improve final answer selection under compute budgets.
method: Three-stage calibration analysis: pre-CoT, intra-CoT, and post-CoT confidence.
Compare SFT, RL, and on-policy distillation (OPD) on mathematical reasoning benchmarks.
Introduce PosConf, which only uses confidence from reliable relative-position intervals.
evidence_signal: Controlled benchmark evaluation shows PosConf improves RL aggregation by 6.1 points over majority voting and improves OPD early stopping by up to 4.3 points under tight token budgets.
watch_item: Position-dependent calibration can invert later in the reasoning trace, so using confidence without stage/position filtering may degrade performance.
paper_title: Post-Training Shifts Confidence: A Three-Stage Analysis of How SFT, RL, and OPD Shape Pre-, Intra-, and Post-CoT Calibration
